acceptable_evidence: Immutable environment definition identifying operating system, compiler, make, architecture, and dependencies
Successful build of the unmodified locked archive using official instructions
Hashes and linked-runtime summaries for both executables
A clean rebuild that reproduces the executable identities or documents controlled variance
blocking_prerequisites.description: No documented NBIS build environment was already present, and installation was outside this audit's authorization.
blocking_prerequisites.evidence: Native gcc, gmake, Cygwin, container runtimes, and installed WSL distributions were absent.
blocking_prerequisites.exact_unresolved_question: Can official NBIS 5.0.0 be built unmodified and reproducibly in a frozen supported environment?
prohibited_shortcuts: installing tools as part of this audit
blocking_prerequisites.resolution_criterion: Both official executables build from the locked archive in a frozen environment and their identities are recorded.
blocking_prerequisites.why_it_blocks_integration: Executable identity, full image-to-score behavior, and runtime failures cannot be established without a successful unmodified build.

acceptable_evidence: An authenticated official NIST downsampler with immutable identity and applicable terms, or an independently reviewable exact implementation of SP 500-289
Conformance evidence covering sigma, radius, odd-row/odd-column selection, borders, odd dimensions, output dimensions, and pixel values
blocking_prerequisites.description: The official 1000-to-500 guidance is clear, but its implementation is not present in the locked NBIS 5.0.0 archive.
blocking_prerequisites.evidence: NISTIR 7839, SP 500-289, SP 500-306, and an exhaustive text search of the acquired source tree.
blocking_prerequisites.exact_unresolved_question: Which immutable implementation will supply the NIST 1000-to-500 PPI operation and how will conformance be proven?
prohibited_shortcuts: treating a later NIST reference as if it were in NBIS 5.0.0
blocking_prerequisites.resolution_criterion: A locked, reviewable implementation passes pixel-level conformance tests for the official method.
blocking_prerequisites.why_it_blocks_integration: MINDTCT does not internally convert the 1000 PPI PNG to its intended 500 PPI operating scale.

acceptable_evidence: Explicit NIST guidance applicable to 2000 PPI operational preprocessing, or
A separately approved scientific validation plan and results demonstrating a fair canonical path for SD300C
blocking_prerequisites.description: No official 2000 PPI operational path was found in the audited NIST sources.
blocking_prerequisites.evidence: The 2000 PPI mentions in SP 500-306 concern aligned reference scans, while the conformance method remains 1000-to-500 PPI.
blocking_prerequisites.exact_unresolved_question: What canonical, scientifically defensible resolution policy should transform or handle SD300C 2000 PPI images?
prohibited_shortcuts: direct experimental use merely to see whether it runs
bespoke enhancement, crop, inversion, or resize
blocking_prerequisites.resolution_criterion: An approved policy identifies the exact operation, implementation, parameters, validation evidence, and fairness rationale for SD300C.
blocking_prerequisites.why_it_blocks_integration: A protocol release cannot be processed through an unsupported or inferred resolution path.

acceptable_evidence: Three fresh-process repetitions on only the authorized fixture after all preceding gates resolve
Stable minutiae payload hashes and BOZORTH3 score payload hashes without committing score values
blocking_prerequisites.description: Runtime determinism has not been measured because the authorization gates did not pass.
blocking_prerequisites.evidence: The probe record is intentionally NOT_RUN.
blocking_prerequisites.exact_unresolved_question: Are MINDTCT representations and BOZORTH3 score payloads deterministic across fresh processes, paths, order, and fixed locale?
blocking_prerequisites.resolution_criterion: The authorized three-repetition probe passes all deterministic-output criteria after build and PPI gates resolve.
blocking_prerequisites.why_it_blocks_integration: Source inspection cannot replace executable-level evidence for reproducible outputs.

build_and_portability_risks.description: Official documentation targets gcc/gmake and reports Linux, macOS, and Cygwin testing; current modern toolchain compatibility remains unmeasured.
build_and_portability_risks.evidence: Official product page and release installation files.
build_and_portability_risks.resolution_criterion: The frozen build gate records all warnings and portability decisions without changing algorithmic source.

licensing_and_provenance_risks.description: NIST identifies NBIS as public domain, but downstream packaging must retain provenance and must not substitute third-party builds.
licensing_and_provenance_risks.evidence: Official NBIS product page and locked official archive.
licensing_and_provenance_risks.resolution_criterion: Runtime package records the official archive and local build identity.

non_blocking_risks.description: BOZORTH3 documents a default top-minutiae limit and an equal-quality cutoff whose order may be undefined.
non_blocking_risks.evidence: Official BOZORTH3 manual in the locked source tree.
non_blocking_risks.resolution_criterion: The determinism probe confirms stable effective input and score payloads for the authorized fixture.

resolution_risks.description: PNG PPI metadata is not propagated by the NBIS 5.0.0 decoder, and MINDTCT falls back to 500 PPI without changing image dimensions.
resolution_risks.evidence: Locked decoder and MINDTCT source files.
resolution_risks.resolution_criterion: Every protocol release enters MINDTCT only through its approved canonical resolution path.

scientific_validity_risks.description: An unsupported 2000 PPI preprocessing choice could change minutiae geometry and invalidate cross-matcher fairness.
scientific_validity_risks.evidence: Absence of an applicable policy in the audited official-source set.
scientific_validity_risks.resolution_criterion: Resolve NBIS_2000_PPI_PREPROCESSING_POLICY_V1 before any SD300C processing.
